required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on April 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: Maintenance Olaparib in BRCA1-Mutated High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma Following Complete Response to Platinum-Based Chemotherapy

brief_summary.textblock: This study evaluates maintenance olaparib (300 mg twice daily) in patients with Stage III or IV high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC) harboring germline BRCA1 pathogenic variants who achieved complete response or partial response after platinum-based adjuvant chemotherapy following primary debulking surgery. The primary endpoint is progression-free survival. HRD-positive (genomic instability score greater than or equal to 42) patients are the target population.

detailed_description.textblock: Eligible patients must have undergone primary debulking surgery with optimal cytoreduction (residual disease R0 or R1 less than 1 cm) followed by 6 cycles of carboplatin and paclitaxel. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant must be confirmed by clinical-grade testing. HRD positivity (genomic instability score by myChoice CDx or equivalent assay) is required. Patients begin olaparib maintenance within 8 weeks of completing chemotherapy. Maintenance continues for up to 2 years. CA-125 normalization after chemotherapy is required. Patients with depression or anxiety managed with antidepressants are eligible. Iron deficiency managed with oral supplementation does not constitute an exclusion.

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Histologically confirmed high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSC), fallopian tube carcinoma, or primary peritoneal carcinoma
- Stage IIIC or IV disease (FIGO 2014 staging)
- Germline BRCA1 pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant confirmed by clinical genetic testing
- HRD positive: genomic instability score greater than or equal to 42 on validated assay (myChoice CDx or equivalent)
- Primary debulking surgery performed with optimal cytoreduction (R0 or residual disease less than 1 cm)
- Completed 6 cycles of platinum-based chemotherapy (carboplatin/paclitaxel or equivalent) with response (complete response or partial response by CA-125 and imaging)
- CA-125 within normal limits at end of chemotherapy
- Olaparib initiation within 8 weeks of last platinum-based chemotherapy dose
- Age 18 to 70 years
- ECOG performance status 0 or 1
- Adequate organ function: ANC greater than or equal to 1.5, platelets greater than or equal to 100, creatinine less than or equal to 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT less than or equal to 2x ULN, hemoglobin greater than or equal to 10 g/dL
- Iron deficiency and mild depression/anxiety managed with medications permitted
Exclusion Criteria:
- Germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (without BRCA1 co-mutation)
- MSI-H tumor status (these patients should be considered for immunotherapy trials)
- HRD-negative (genomic instability score less than 42)
- Suboptimal cytoreduction (residual disease greater than 1 cm after debulking)
- Prior PARP inhibitor therapy
- Prior platinum-resistant disease (progression within 6 months of prior platinum)
- Myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukemia
- Active or uncontrolled hepatic disease
- Diabetes mellitus or hypertension requiring multiple medications (metabolic comorbidities that may confound safety assessment)
- Interval debulking surgery (neoadjuvant approach) — only primary debulking eligible
- Mucinous, clear cell, or endometrioid histology
